mainEntity.name: How much does a Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 3.6 TDV8 engine typically costs £4,000–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A supply-and-fit service from some specialists is available from approximately £3,995. Main dealer new engines start from £12,000–£18,000+ supply only.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 3.6 TDV8 is a robust engine with a cast iron block and strong bottom end. However, it has well-documented weaknesses: turbocharger VGT mechanism failure, valve corrosion, and timing chain tensioner wear. With meticulous maintenance and proactive turbo care, many owners achieve high mileages — but these issues should be budgeted for.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Turbocharger VGT mechanism failure — the most common and costly issue, (2) Valve corrosion — a known metallurgical issue requiring cylinder head work, and (3) Timing chain tensioner wear — can cause catastrophic engine failure if ignored.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £5,000–£12,000 Range Rover Sport, a £4,000–£6,500 recon is 30–130% of the car's value. For a clean example with good body and chassis, replacement may be worthwhile given the L320's strong following. However, if the vehicle has significant corrosion or air suspension issues, evaluate total restoration costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 3.6 TDV8 uses the 368DT engine code (also known as LION). It's a 3.6-litre twin-turbo V8 diesel producing 272 PS and 640 Nm. The engine number is stamped on the cylinder block in two locations. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent turbo failure on the 3.6 TDV8?

acceptedAnswer.text: Prevention is not guaranteed, but you can reduce the risk: (1) Change oil every 10,000 miles or less with the correct 5W-30 JLR-spec oil — turbochargers are oil-dependent, (2) Allow the engine to idle for 30–60 seconds before switching off to let the turbos cool, (3) Use high-quality diesel fuel, and (4) Never ignore restricted performance warnings or siren-like noises — book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.title: Land Rover Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 3.6-litre twin-turbo V8 diesel — cast iron block, chain-driven. Turbocharger and valve corrosion are main weaknesses

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 engine typically takes 15–25 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The exhaust manifold gasket replacement is particularly labour-intensive as it requires body removal.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Turbocharger Failure (Geometry Mechanism)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, restricted performance warning, siren-like whining or whistling from the engine bay, black smoke under acceleration, engine management light
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,000 (turbo rebuild or replacement — both turbos recommended)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if you hear siren-like noise

problems.recommendation: The 3.6 TDV8 is fitted with two BorgWarner BV39 turbochargers. These turbos are generally robust, but the variable geometry mechanism is a known weak point that can fail. Symptoms include a siren-like noise, loss of power, and restricted performance warnings. Because this is a V8 with two turbos, replacement is labour-intensive and expensive. Many specialists recommend replacing both turbos at the same time to avoid repeat failure. If turbo failure is ignored, debris can enter the engine and cause catastrophic damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full turbo failure guide → /failures/tdv8-turbo-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Valve Corrosion
problems.symptoms: Rough running, misfires, loss of compression, poor cold starting, increased smoke
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if running issues develop

problems.recommendation: The 3.6 TDV8 has a known issue with the metallurgical composition of its valves, which can become heavily corroded over time. This is a significant job — the cylinder heads must be removed, valves replaced, and the heads rebuilt. The repair is expensive but not as catastrophic as a full engine replacement. This is a known pattern on higher-mileage examples and should be factored into ownership costs.

failureLink.label: Read the full valve corrosion guide → /failures/tdv8-valve-corrosion/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.symptoms: Rattling or clanking noise from the front of the engine on cold start, engine warning light, rough running

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling on cold start, book an inspection immediately

problems.recommendation: The 3.6 TDV8 uses a chain-driven timing system. Timing chain tensioner failure is a documented issue on these engines. If the tensioner fails, the chain can become slack and potentially snap, causing catastrophic engine damage. A TSB (LTB00474) exists for this issue, specifying that both pairs of tensioners and blades must be replaced. At the first sign of cold-start rattling, book an inspection immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain tensioner guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Full engine replacement is the only reliable fix

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with 10,000-mile or less oil change intervals
* Evidence of recent turbo work — this is a major expense
* No cold-start rattle — timing chain health is critical
* Good body and chassis condition — these are ageing vehicles now
avoidIf: * Any cold-start rattle — this is a warning sign of timing chain tensioner wear
* Siren-like noise from the engine — indicates turbo failure
* Restricted performance warnings — could indicate turbo or other issues
* No service history — the TDV8 is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 3.6 TDV8 is a genuinely impressive engine — a 272 PS twin-turbo V8 diesel with 640 Nm of torque that transformed the Range Rover Sport's performance. The cast iron block and robust bottom end make it a durable unit, but the turbochargers, valve corrosion, and timing chain tensioners are well-documented weaknesses. Buy with full service history and budget for turbo maintenance. Without history? Approach with caution — the repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport 3.6 TDV8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or clanking from the front of the engine (timing chain tensioner)
* Turbo Noise — Listen for siren-like whining or whistling under acceleration
* Restricted Performance — Any warning messages? This often indicates turbo issues
* Service History — Including the 368DT engine and confirmed oil change intervals (10,000 miles or less)
* Smoke Check — Black smoke (turbo) or blue smoke (valve / oil issues)
* Exhaust Manifold — Any exhaust noise? The gasket is a known weak point
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* Body Condition — Exhaust manifold gasket replacement requires body removal; factor this into any work
